skill.name: postmark-automation
skill.description: Automate Postmark email delivery tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): send templated emails, manage templates, monitor delivery stats and bounces. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Send templated emails, manage templates, and monitor delivery stats through Postmark's API via Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 63 external command patterns and 5 network patterns. All external command findings are false positives - the detected backticks are markdown code formatting in documentation (SKILL.md), not Ruby shell execution. Network findings represent legitimate Postmark API usage via Rube MCP. The skill requires users to set up their own Rube MCP connection and authenticate with Postmark. No hardcoded credentials or malicious patterns detected.

low_findings.title: External Network Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill requires connection to external Rube MCP server (https://rube.app/mcp) for Postmark API access. This is a legitimate third-party service but introduces external dependency.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct reference to external MCP endpoint required for skill functionality - legitimate dependency but should be documented.

content.value_statement: Send templated emails, manage templates, and monitor delivery stats through Postmark's API via Rube MCP. Streamline your transactional email workflows without manual API calls.
actual_capabilities: Send batch templated emails to multiple recipients using Postmark templates
Create, edit, and validate email templates with variable substitution
Monitor delivery statistics including sent, opened, clicked, and bounced counts
Retrieve and manage bounce records and spam complaints
View and configure Postmark server settings including webhooks and tracking
Search and list available templates with pagination support
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with Postmark toolkit configured
Batch send limited to 500 messages per API call
Sender addresses must be verified Sender Signatures or from verified domains in Postmark
Does not handle SMTP relay - uses Postmark API only

use_cases.title: Startup Notification System
use_cases.description: Automate welcome emails, password resets, and account notifications using Postmark templates. Set up triggered email sequences for user onboarding flows.
use_cases.target_user: Startup founders and developers building user-facing applications

use_cases.title: E-commerce Order Communications
use_cases.description: Send order confirmations, shipping updates, and delivery notifications. Monitor bounce rates to maintain sender reputation and reduce failed deliveries.

use_cases.title: SaaS Product Updates
use_cases.description: Manage template-based announcements for feature releases, maintenance notices, and billing updates. Track open rates to measure engagement.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers and marketing teams at SaaS companies

prompt_templates.title: Send a Simple Templated Email
prompt_templates.prompt: Send an email using the Postmark template with ID 123456 to recipient@example.com. Use the template model with name set to 'John' and order_id set to 'ORD-789'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic single-recipient email using an existing template

prompt_templates.title: Batch Send with Multiple Recipients
prompt_templates.prompt: Send a batch of emails using template alias 'welcome-email' to these 50 new users. Each recipient should have their name and signup_date populated from this CSV data.
prompt_templates.scenario: Sending personalized onboarding emails to multiple new users

prompt_templates.title: Check Delivery Health
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the delivery statistics for the past 7 days. Show me the bounce breakdown by type and identify any spam complaints. Also pull the outbound overview with open and click rates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Monitoring email deliverability and sender reputation

prompt_templates.title: Create and Validate a New Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Postmark template called 'Password Reset' with subject 'Reset your password' and this HTML body. Then validate it with sample data showing username and reset_link variables.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a new email template and testing variable rendering

output_examples.output: Retrieved 12 templates: Welcome Email (ID: 1001), Order Confirmation (ID: 1002), Password Reset (ID: 1003), Shipping Notification (ID: 1004), and 8 more.

output_examples.input: Get delivery stats for last week
output_examples.output: Delivery Summary (7 days): Sent: 2,450 | Opened: 1,823 (74.4%) | Clicked: 892 (36.4%) | Bounced: 27 (1.1%). Bounce breakdown: HardBounce: 15, SoftBounce: 10, SpamComplaint: 2.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Validate templates with sample data using POSTMARK_VALIDATE_TEMPLATE before sending to catch missing variables
Monitor bounce and spam complaint rates regularly to maintain sender reputation and remove hard-bounced addresses
anti_patterns: Do not send batch emails exceeding 500 messages in a single call - split into multiple batches
Do not use unverified sender addresses - emails will fail delivery
Do not ignore hard bounces - repeatedly sending to invalid addresses damages sender reputation

faq.question: How do I connect Postmark to Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'postmark'. If the connection is not active, follow the authentication link provided to complete OAuth setup with your Postmark account.

faq.question: What is the maximum number of emails I can send in one batch?
faq.answer: Postmark limits batch sends to 500 messages per API call. For larger lists, split your recipients into multiple batches of 500 or fewer.

faq.question: How do I find my template ID?
faq.answer: Use POSTMARK_LIST_TEMPLATES to retrieve all templates with their IDs and names. Template IDs are numeric integers used for GET, EDIT, and SEND operations.

faq.question: What happens if my template has missing variables?
faq.answer: Use POSTMARK_VALIDATE_TEMPLATE with sample TemplateModel data before sending. This catches missing or mismatched variable names early, preventing send failures.

faq.question: Can I track opens and clicks on my emails?
faq.answer: Yes, configure TrackOpens and TrackLinks in server settings via POSTMARK_GET_SERVER and POSTMARK_EDIT_SERVER. Use POSTMARK_GET_OUTBOUND_OVERVIEW to view aggregated statistics.

faq.question: How do I handle bounced email addresses?
faq.answer: Use POSTMARK_GET_BOUNCES to retrieve bounce records. Remove hard-bounced addresses from your mailing list immediately. Soft bounces may be temporary; monitor for patterns.
